solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int findTheLongestSubstring(String s) {
        int[] maskIndex = new int[32];
        Arrays.fill(maskIndex, -1);
        maskIndex[0] = 0;
        int mask = 0, maxLen = 0;
        int[] vowelBits = new int[26];
        vowelBits['a' - 'a'] = 1 << 0;
        vowelBits['e' - 'a'] = 1 << 1;
        vowelBits['i' - 'a'] = 1 << 2;
        vowelBits['o' - 'a'] = 1 << 3;
        vowelBits['u' - 'a'] = 1 << 4;

        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            char c = s.charAt(i);
            if (vowelBits[c - 'a'] != 0) {
                mask ^= vowelBits[c - 'a'];
            }
            int index = mask;
            if (maskIndex[index] != -1) {
                maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, i + 1 - maskIndex[index]);
            } else {
                maskIndex[index] = i + 1;
            }
        }
        return maxLen;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses a bitmask to track the parity (even/odd count) of vowels encountered so far. It maps each vowel to a bit in an integer mask and updates the mask as it iterates through the string. By storing the first occurrence of each mask, it efficiently finds the longest valid substring with even counts of all vowels. The approach is optimal for large input sizes.>
